title: Cum au câștigat liberalii olandezi – lecții pentru USR
summary: The article analyzes how the Dutch social-liberal party D66 rebuilt itself after a massive electoral defeat in 2023 by embracing radical honesty, empathy, and executive optimism. It draws direct parallels to Romania's USR party, arguing it faces the same crossroads and should adopt D66's blueprint of mission-based governance, fiscal reform, and citizen-centered administration.
talking_points: D66 lost over 60% of its parliamentary seats in 2023 and chose radical self-criticism over blame-shifting, publicly admitting its elitist and technocratic disconnect from ordinary citizens
The party adopted a 'Het kan wel' (It is possible) philosophy, shifting from defensive rhetoric to solution-oriented governance that reconciles economic liberalism with social protection

D66 proposed replacing siloed ministries with cross-cutting 'mission ministries', digitalizing public administration to reduce corruption, and granting local governments autonomy to deviate from national regulations

The fiscal reform plan centered on reducing income tax on low and middle earners while increasing taxes on pollution and large wealth, and declaring education and research budgets as untouchable long-term investments
USR in Romania faces the same stagnation and must undertake a similar process of radical honesty and programmatic reinvention
author: Mihai Gabriel Costea
